steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" dialog.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click on the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface at coordinates (288, 101).
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Click the "Turn on desktop notifications" button in the banner at the top of the Microsoft Teams interface.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
file_path='/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_NTADp/env/.env.local.user'
if [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  sed -i 's/crypto_/crypto_fff/' "$file_path"
else
  echo "File does not exist: $file_path"
  exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while interacting with the "Debug in Microsoft 365 Agents Playground" notification dialog.
Type text in Visual Studio Code's Command Palette: '>Debug:Select and Start Debugging', targeting the 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' option highlighted in blue.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.

Type text: 'Debug in Teams(Chrome)' in Visual Studio Code's "Run and Debug" dropdown menu to select the debugging configuration, highlighted with the label "Debug in Teams(Chrome)" under the "Most Recent" section.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option from the dropdown menu in the "Run and Debug" section of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Report Issue" button in the error prompt located at the bottom-right corner of the Visual Studio Code interface within the OUTPUT panel.
@code 'Create new issue' is exist in web page
